extraction.one_line_summary: GANNA Filippo won the 14km opening ITT by 24 seconds over EVENEPOEL, with POGACAR third showing ominous form just days after a 49km solo win at Strade Bianche.
tactical_patterns: GANNA aggressively hit apexes nearly clipping barriers at mid-course turn, prioritizing speed over safety margin for 24-second margin.
POGACAR dominated GC rivals but finished 24 seconds behind pure TTers, revealing time trial specialists maintain advantage on short flat courses.
EVENEPOEL lost 9 seconds in second half after being only 2 seconds down at turn, suggesting power fade on return leg.
ASGREEN established 2-hour hot seat benchmark before favorites, showing depth in time trial at non-ideal 14km distance versus his 60km sweet spot.

rider_signals.GANNA Filippo: positive: Took 24 seconds from Evenepoel on 14km flat course despite ungainly zigzagging around manholes suggesting raw power overcame technical inefficiency
rider_signals.POGACAR Tadej: positive: Finished third just days after 49km solo Strade Bianche break showing no residual fatigue from extreme weekend effort
rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: neutral: Lost 9 seconds in final 7km after trailing only 2 seconds at turn indicating second-half power sustainability issue
rider_signals.GEOGHEGAN HART Tao: neutral: Finished 17 seconds behind Dowsett benchmark revealing unclear INEOS leadership with competitive internal dynamics
rider_signals.DOWSETT Alex: positive: Held hot seat for two hours and finished fifth overall as non-GC rider showing exceptional pacing strategy
